| AC-2.5 | RLS: **the FULL §4.3 admin-only table list is denied to non-admin sessions across all four verbs (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE)**, including (`shows_internal`, `pending_syncs`, `pending_ingestions`, `sync_audit`, `crew_member_auth`, `revoked_links`) and the complete spec §4.3 list — `bootstrap_nonces`, `pending_snapshot_uploads`, `revision_race_cooldowns`, `wizard_finalize_checkpoints`, `shows_pending_changes`, `recovery_drift_cooldowns`, etc. The AC list MUST be derived FROM the §4.3 admin-only contract (not hand-rolled), so any addition to §4.3 automatically grows AC-2.5. A non-admin Supabase Auth session MUST receive an RLS denial (zero rows for SELECT; permission-denied / zero-affected-rows for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) for EACH of the following tables: `shows_internal`, `sync_log`, `reports`, `pending_syncs`, `pending_ingestions`, `crew_member_auth`, `revoked_links`, `link_sessions`, `bootstrap_nonces`, `app_settings`, `email_deliveries`, `deferred_ingestions`, `admin_alerts`, `sync_audit`, `drive_watch_channels`, `report_rate_limits`, `onboarding_scan_manifest`, `pending_snapshot_uploads`, `revision_race_cooldowns`, `validation_state`, `wizard_finalize_checkpoints`, `shows_pending_changes`, `recovery_drift_cooldowns`. The test exercises every (table × verb) cell — **23 tables × 4 verbs = 92 assertions** (prose track; live `admin-rls-runtime.test.ts` track = 19 × 4 = 76 per the §4.3 footnote — the M11.5 G3 cutover filter on retired M9.5 tables explains the delta) — using the `__test_singleton_rls_probe` SECURITY-INVOKER helper that runs each verb under a non-admin Supabase Auth session and reports PostgREST/PostgreSQL denial behavior. Per-table assertions cover all 4 verbs; missing any cell fails the test. The §4.3 admin-only list is the SINGLE source of truth for this matrix; if §4.3 grows, AC-2.5 grows automatically (the test reads the §4.3 list — Task 2.5 introspection matrix — rather than hardcoding the table names). **Build-time invariant (X.6 audit)**: an additional cross-cutting assertion compares the count and identity of tables enumerated in this AC against the §4.3 admin-only contract; the build fails if the two lists drift (e.g., a new admin-only table added to §4.3 but missed in AC-2.5, or vice versa). | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |

| AC-4.5 | Empty-state discipline (per-field): a fixture with `Opening Reel = TBD` does NOT render an `Opening reel:` line on the crew page. Fixtures with `Opening Reel ∈ {YES, MAYBE, N/A, TBA, BACKUP ONLY}` DO render the status line `Opening reel: <value>` per §10. **A mixed-value fixture `YES - https://drive.google.com/file/d/<id>/view` renders the status line `Opening reel: YES` (URL-stripped) — NOT the raw cell value.** The §10 render contract strips all `https?://drive.google.com\|docs.google.com/...` substrings AND orphaned `-` connector tokens BEFORE the line is rendered, so the raw `https://drive.google.com/...` URL MUST NOT appear anywhere in crew DOM at any milestone. **Regression assertion (M4 baseline AND M7)**: for every fixture whose `event_details.opening_reel` cell contains `https://`, the crew-page DOM must satisfy `await expect(page.locator('[data-testid=opening-reel-tile]').textContent()).not.toContain('https://')` AND `await expect(page.locator('main').textContent()).not.toContain('drive.google.com')`. **At M4 the crew page MUST NOT render any `<video>` element**: `/api/asset/reel/<show>` is created by Task 7.6 in M7, so M4 cannot stream reel bytes. The inline `<video>` rendering is added in M7 and verified by AC-7.25 — until then, every opening-reel cell renders as URL-stripped text only. A pure-URL cell (`https://drive.google.com/file/d/<id>/view` with no surrounding text) renders NO `Opening reel:` line at M4 (the entire cell value is the URL; stripping leaves empty residue, which the empty-state predicate hides) AND renders ONLY the inline `<video>` at M7. Generic optional fields (e.g., `event_details.power`) hide on `TBD`/`N/A`/`TBA`/empty and render verbatim otherwise — only `opening_reel` carries the §10 named-status exception AND the URL-strip render rule. | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |

| AC-5.3 | (single host-wide cookie + show-id-bound JSON payload; per-show cookie naming is rejected because browsers send all matching `(domain, path)` cookies regardless of name) **Cross-show A→B→A burn pattern:** there is exactly ONE `__Host-fxav_session` cookie on the host at any time; its value is the URL-encoded JSON payload `{ token, show_id }` minted at redemption. Opening a signed link for show A → cookie holds `show_id = A`. Navigating to `/show/<show-b-slug>` → §7.2.2 step 4 reads the cookie, sees `cookie.show_id !== B`, returns `{ kind: 'continue', clearCookie: true }` AND DELETEs the show-A `link_sessions` row; the chain falls through to Google/admin. Navigating back to `/show/<show-a-slug>` → no cookie present (was cleared on the show-B detour), chain falls through to Google/admin; if neither succeeds, the user must re-open show-A's original signed link to re-mint. **This burn cost is acceptable for v1** — multi-show navigation is rare; future versions may upgrade to an array-cookie design that avoids the burn. Required regression test: redeem signed link for show A → assert `__Host-fxav_session` cookie value decodes to `{ token, show_id: A }` AND `link_sessions` row exists with `show_id = A` AND token matches; navigate to `/show/<show-b-slug>` → assert response Set-Cookie clears `__Host-fxav_session` AND the `link_sessions` row for show-A's token has been DELETEd AND the chain fell through to Google/admin (or 401 redirect-to-bootstrap for show B if no other credentials present); navigate back to `/show/<show-a-slug>` → assert no `__Host-fxav_session` cookie present AND chain falls through (or 401 redirect-to-bootstrap if Google/admin also absent). Row-PRESERVE on step 4 is rejected because the cookie is unrecoverable from the client once cleared, making row-preserve pointless. Per-show NAMING (claiming "TWO distinct cookies coexist") is also rejected because the underlying browser-cookie model (cookies indexed by `(domain, path)`, sent regardless of name) makes per-show naming both ineffective for isolation and a Cookie-header growth vector. | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |

| AC-6.11 | First-seen sheet flow (per amendment 9, ratified 2026-05-09): drop a brand-new sheet into the folder, run cron. Behavior splits on parse outcome: (a) MI-1..MI-5b fail → `pending_ingestions` row with the relevant MI hard-fail code; no `shows` row created. (b) MI-6..MI-14 trip → `pending_syncs` row with the relevant MI sentinel (e.g., `MI-6_CREW_SHRINKAGE`); no `FIRST_SEEN_REVIEW` sentinel emitted. (c) ALL MI-1..MI-14 pass → Phase 2 auto-applies, `shows` row exists, `unpublish_token IS NOT NULL`, `unpublish_token_expires_at = now() + 24h`, `SHOW_FIRST_PUBLISHED` admin_alerts row exists, no `pending_syncs` row. The 24h undo path (M12.13 `token`+`r` contract): `POST /api/show/[slug]/unpublish?token=<valid>&r=<recipient-binding>` — or the `/show/[slug]/unpublish` confirm page, which consumes through the same locked wrapper — archives the show, revokes crew links, consumes the token. Bare slug+token POSTs are rejected with a neutral 404 (no code in the body, token NOT consumed). Subsequent calls with consumed tokens return the neutral 404 without a code (once the mint is gone the recipient binding is underivable; `UNPUBLISH_TOKEN_CONSUMED` is in-app-only); calls past 24h with a valid `r` return 400 `UNPUBLISH_TOKEN_EXPIRED`. Onboarding-scan first-seen (`mode='onboarding_scan'`) still stages with `ONBOARDING_SCAN_REVIEW` (separate AC for the wizard flow). | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |

| AC-7.24 | Apply-time reel drift (single 410 contract; 4-column propagation): stage a parse with a reel URL. EDIT the reel file in Drive (`headRevisionId` advances). Click Apply. The Apply-time re-verify (§6.11.1's four-step flow) detects drift via `headRevisionId` mismatch, persists ALL FOUR `opening_reel_*` columns as NULL atomically (`opening_reel_drive_file_id`, `opening_reel_drive_modified_time`, `opening_reel_head_revision_id`, AND `opening_reel_mime_type` — `opening_reel_mime_type` is the 4th pin column gating inline `<video>` rendering on `mimeType.startsWith('video/')`; partial-NULL states are forbidden), emits `REEL_DRIFTED` warning. Crew page falls back to text-only display. **Single 410 contract**: `/api/asset/reel/<show>` returns 410 whenever ANY of the FOUR persisted pin columns is NULL OR live drift is detected — the same 410 status covers (a) all-four-NULL after Apply-time drift, (b) `mime_type` NULL covering the `OPENING_REEL_NOT_VIDEO` branch (operator pasted a Google Doc / Slides / image / PDF URL — substring extractor resolved a `driveFileId` but enrichment detected non-video MIME and persisted all four columns NULL atomically per the §4.1 column note), and (c) live drift. The page knows not to call the route when any column is NULL, but the route still fails closed if hit. **MIME-driven nulling assertion**: a fixture with `event_details.opening_reel = 'https://docs.google.com/document/d/<id>/edit'` (Google Doc URL) MUST produce ALL FOUR `shows.opening_reel_*` columns NULL after Apply, MUST emit `OPENING_REEL_NOT_VIDEO` to `parse_warnings`, AND `/api/asset/reel/<show>` MUST return 410 if hit. | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |
| AC-7.25 | Inline `<video>` rendering lands in M7 (URL-strip + 4-column propagation): for every fixture whose post-Apply `shows.opening_reel_drive_file_id`, `shows.opening_reel_drive_modified_time`, `shows.opening_reel_head_revision_id`, AND `shows.opening_reel_mime_type` are ALL non-NULL (`opening_reel_mime_type` is the 4th pin column AND requires `mime_type.startsWith('video/')` — a non-video MIME persists all four columns NULL atomically per AC-7.24, so the all-four-non-NULL gate by construction also implies video MIME), the crew page MUST render exactly one `<video>` element with `src="/api/asset/reel/<show>"`. For mixed-value cells like `YES - https://drive.google.com/file/d/<id>/view`, the page renders BOTH the URL-stripped text status line `Opening reel: YES` (M4's URL-stripped text baseline from AC-4.5 is preserved) AND the inline `<video>` (M7 additively adds the `<video>`). For pure-URL cells (entire cell value is the Drive URL, residue empty after stripping), M7 renders ONLY the inline `<video>` — no `Opening reel:` text line. For text-only cells (`MAYBE`, `YES`, `N/A`, etc. — no URL substring) AND for cells with `parseResult.openingReel = null` AND for shows whose pin columns are NULL after Apply-time drift (`REEL_DRIFTED` warning fired per AC-7.24) AND for shows whose `opening_reel_mime_type` is NULL (non-video MIME — `OPENING_REEL_NOT_VIDEO` warning fired; per AC-7.24 all four columns are NULL atomically in this case, so this collapses to the all-NULL branch), the page renders text only — no `<video>` element. **Crew DOM MUST NEVER contain `https://` or `drive.google.com` substrings** for any opening-reel fixture at any milestone — the URL-strip render contract enforces this. This AC supersedes AC-4.5 for M7 onwards: the M4 assertion `video[src*="/api/asset/reel/"]` count === 0 inverts here to count === 1 for the same fixture once Task 7.6 ships. Test harness scopes the `<video>` count to `[data-testid=opening-reel-tile]` to avoid collision with diagram-gallery `<video>` elements (none exist in v1, but the scope guards against future media tiles). **MIME-driven render-gating assertion**: a fixture with a non-video Drive URL (e.g., `application/vnd.google-apps.document`, `application/vnd.google-apps.presentation`, `image/png`, `application/pdf`) MUST render NO `<video>` element at M7 (because all four pin columns are NULL after Apply per AC-7.24's MIME gate); a fixture with `video/mp4` MUST render exactly one `<video>` element. | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |

At M4 the same pure-URL cell renders NEITHER the text line NOR a `<video>` element (empty stripped residue + no asset route). `MAYBE` renders as a text line at every milestone. `YES - <url>` renders the URL-stripped text line `Opening reel: YES` (M4 baseline) AND the inline `<video>` (M7 addition). **Crew DOM MUST NEVER contain `https://` or `drive.google.com` substrings** for any opening-reel fixture — the URL-strip render contract enforces this at every milestone. | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |

RETURNING count` guarantees no race-through. | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |

| AC-X.1 | §12.4 catalog ↔ runtime parity (full row payload).** Build-time generator parses §12.4 prose into `SPEC_CODES` (active) + `RETIRED_CODES` (retired) carrying the full row payload (`dougFacing`/`crewFacing`/`followUp`/`helpfulContext`); runtime catalog (`lib/errors/catalog.ts`) is loaded; the parity test asserts (a) field-by-field deep equality of every active code, (b) every retired code is absent from runtime, (c) duplicate-active dedup invariant uses the full row hash as key, (d) every code reachable from at least one fixture or synthesized scenario via the code-to-scenario registry. Failure emits named diffs (`+missing_in_runtime:CODE`, `-extra_in_runtime:CODE`, field-level `dougFacing differs for CODE`). NOT a flat `git grep` set comparison. | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |
| AC-X.2 | No raw error codes in user-visible surfaces — AST audit + DOM property crawl.** Static analysis covers ACTIVE and RETIRED codes across `components/**/*.tsx` AND user-visible attribute values (`aria-label`, `title`, `alt`, `placeholder`, `value`); JSXAttribute-aware AST analysis (NOT regex over text). **Plus a runtime Playwright crawl** that reads each rendered surface's live DOM property values (`inputElement.value`, `<select>.selectedOptions[i].text`, `contenteditable` regions' `innerText`, shadow-DOM children) — not just `textContent`. A code present in any of these emit-points fails the audit. | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |
| AC-X.3 | Auth-chain every protected sink runs AFTER terminal-validator success on every control-flow path.** Static analysis classifies every route by trust domain (crew-session / admin / me / auth-library / public-bootstrap / public-webhook / cron-internal / server-action / non-route); AST control-flow + dominator analysis with cross-import call graph asserts the validator chain (B1 admin-precedence, B2 link-wins, B3 link-continue → google, B4 link-continue → google-continue → admin) runs to a terminal `success` discriminator BEFORE any `PROTECTED_SINKS` member fires. **Outcome-discriminator audit (`verifyOutcomeDiscriminators`)**: every validator's `kind === 'success' \| 'continue'` discriminator MUST be inspected before sinks/next-validator. **`PROTECTED_SINKS` table list is GENERATED from spec §4.3 admin-only list at build time** (NOT hand-rolled) — currently 19 admin-only tables; future §4.3 additions auto-propagate. **`AUTH_LIB_ALLOWLIST`** carries the small set of files that legitimately touch auth primitives. **`.rpc(...)` calls are protected-by-default** (empty initial `RPC_ALLOWLIST`); non-literal RPC names are ALWAYS sinks. **`.from(...)` calls with non-literal arguments** (e.g., `from(tableName)` where `tableName` is a parameter, or ``from(`${expr}`)``) are ALWAYS treated as protected sinks unless explicitly allowlisted via `DYNAMIC_FROM_ALLOWLIST` (initially empty; entries require reviewed justification). Without this rule, an attacker (or a refactor) could build a route that does `.from(req.query.t)` and bypass every regex-based table-name check. | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |
| AC-X.4 | No global cursor; gating watermarks are Drive-derived per-row only.** Static analysis (three-layer): (1) no source file references a `lastPollAt`-shape global variable, env var, or table column; (2) sync entry points include `runScheduledCronSync`, `runManualSyncForShow`, `runPushSyncForShow`, `runOnboardingScan`, `retrySingleFile`, `assetRecovery`, `applyStagedParse` (Task 6.11 Apply CAS), and `discardStagedParse` (Task 6.12 Discard CAS); (3) per-row sources are split into `AUTHORITATIVE_GATING_WATERMARKS` (Drive-derived; valid as the RHS of a sync-decision comparison: `shows.last_seen_modified_time`, `pending_syncs.base_modified_time` (the column lives on `pending_syncs`, not `shows`; the Apply-time CAS predicate `shows.last_seen_modified_time IS NOT DISTINCT FROM pending_syncs.base_modified_time` joins live-snapshot to staged-base across the two tables), `shows.diagrams ->> 'snapshot_revision_id'` (no top-level column; the value lives inside the `shows.diagrams` JSONB at JSON path `->> 'snapshot_revision_id'`. Apply-time snapshot-stability checks against the JSONB-path expression, e.g., `(shows.diagrams ->> 'snapshot_revision_id')::uuid = reviewed_revision_id`), `pending_syncs.staged_modified_time`, `pending_syncs.staged_id`, `fileMeta.modifiedTime`/`fileMeta.driveModifiedTime`/`fileMeta.headRevisionId`/`fileMeta.md5Checksum`, `deferred_ingestions.deferred_at_modified_time`, `drive_watch_channels.{expires_at, activated_at}`) and `DISPLAY_ONLY_TIMESTAMPS` (rendered to operator but NEVER read in a sync-decision comparison: `shows.last_synced_at`, `pending_syncs.parsed_at`, `pending_ingestions.last_attempt_at` (real column per §4.1 `create table pending_ingestions` is `last_attempt_at` with no `-ed`), `pending_ingestions.first_seen_at`, `deferred_ingestions.deferred_at`); a sync-decision read of any `DISPLAY_ONLY_TIMESTAMPS` member fails the audit. **The X.4 audit fixture set MUST exercise the JSONB-path form** — e.g., `(shows.diagrams ->> 'snapshot_revision_id')::uuid = reviewed_revision_id` SHOULD pass the audit; the audit recognizes JSONB-path expressions on the allowlist, not just bare column references. Plan Task X.4 audit code (owned by the X.1-X.6 subagent) implements the JSONB-path matching. The matcher rule is **driven from the symbol set itself** — a `BinaryExpression` is in scope iff at least one operand resolves to an `AUTHORITATIVE_GATING_WATERMARKS` member regardless of whether the operand name carries a `modified_time`/`last_synced_at` token, AND the audit performs **per-row UUID-CAS coverage** for `pending_syncs.staged_id` and `(shows.diagrams ->> 'snapshot_revision_id')::uuid` (provenance check + coverage sweep) — not just timestamp-shaped operands. | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |
| AC-X.5 | Email canonicalization at every persistence boundary — full coverage.** Static analysis covers every spec-defined email persistence path including `crew_members.email`, `admin_alerts.context.*email*`, `report_rate_limits.identity` (when prefixed `email:`), `sync_audit.applied_by`, `pending_syncs.wizard_approved_by_email`, `shows_pending_changes.applied_by_email`, `app_settings.{watched_folder_set_by_email, pending_folder_set_by_email}`, `email_deliveries.recipient`, `deferred_ingestions.deferred_by_email`, `admin_alerts.resolved_by`, AND read-side `WHERE email = $x` predicates in `listShowsForCrew` (`/me`) — every site MUST call `canonicalize(email)` or be documented as already-canonical (e.g., from `crew_members.email`). Plan Task X.5's boundary list is the canonical inventory. | — | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |
| AC-X.6 | Spec-to-implementation traceability — machine-generated matrix + cross-cutting build-time invariants.** Generator walks every spec heading + every `<!-- spec-id: ... -->` HTML-comment anchor (no implicit prose-mention fallback); plan tasks include structured `<!-- coverage: §N.M, AC-X.Y, spec-id-slug -->` markers; matrix has columns `Spec anchor \| Title \| Owning task ID(s) \| Status \| Implementation evidence \| Notes`. Status ∈ {`planned`, `implemented`, `deferred`, `intentionally out of scope`, `MISSING`}. CI fails on `MISSING > 0` AND on failure of ANY substantive parity, coverage, or code-producer assertion enumerated in Plan Task X.6 Step 2 (gating only on the `MISSING` count would leave Step-2 parity assertions un-wired into the PR-required check; a spec drift that broke admin-table parity but left every anchor mapped would have a green CI). **The required GitHub status checks for the full X.\* gate set — all PR-required, all blocking merge — are named verbatim**: `traceability-audit` (X.6), `x1-catalog-parity` (X.1), `x2-no-raw-codes` (X.2), `x3-trust-domain` (X.3), `x4-no-global-cursor` (X.4), `x5-email-canonicalization` (X.5), `verify-branch-protection-status` (X.6 drift-detector reader — the 7th required check is the LIGHTWEIGHT READER, NOT the privileged `verify-branch-protection` job itself, because GitHub does not send secrets on `pull_request` from forks (every fork PR would fail closed → permanent merge deadlock for every external contribution) and `pull_request_target` would expose secrets to untrusted PR_HEAD code — a documented security hole. The reader uses only the auto-injected read-only `GITHUB_TOKEN` to query the latest successful run of the privileged `verify-branch-protection` workflow on `main` and asserts it succeeded within an 8-day freshness window. The privileged `verify-branch-protection` job runs ONLY on `push` to `main` + weekly `schedule` cron — both contexts run committed-to-main code with secrets safely available. Recursive-bootstrap property: the privileged script asserts the reader is in the required-checks set (so an admin who removes it triggers drift on the next privileged run); the reader in turn requires the privileged job to have succeeded recently (so breaking the privileged job fails the merge gate within 8 days)), `x6-pg-cron-pivot` (M12.1 cross-cutting pg_cron pivot defenses — the 8th required check, added 2026-05-27). Each is a separate Vitest project / script in `.github/workflows/x-audits.yml`; the audit jobs (traceability-audit, x1–x5, x6-pg-cron-pivot — 7 total post-M12.1) and the lightweight reader (`verify-branch-protection-status`) run on every pull request and every branch build; the privileged `verify-branch-protection` job runs on `push` to `main` + weekly `schedule` cron only. **Post-merge / deploy-only audits are NOT acceptable for any X.\* gate**. Each job uploads its audit artifact (`coverage.md` for X.6; named diffs for X.1–X.5; `branch-protection-report.json` for the privileged verify-branch-protection job) on every run regardless of pass/fail. **Cross-cutting parity assertion**: parse spec §4.3 admin-only bullet list to extract the canonical admin-only table set; parse Plan Task 2.3's `ADMIN_TABLES` registry AND Task X.3's `PROTECTED_SINKS` regex list; assert `setEqual(specAdminTables, ac25AdminTables)` AND `specAdminTables.every(t => protectedSinksRegexList.includes(t))`. Test fails with named diff (`+missing_in_ac25:bootstrap_nonces`) when any list drifts. Future admin-only tables added to §4.3 must auto-propagate to all three lists or CI fails. **Cross-cutting AC-X.4 spec ↔ plan parity**: `AUTHORITATIVE_GATING_WATERMARKS` and `DISPLAY_ONLY_TIMESTAMPS` symbol sets in spec §X.4 prose MUST match those in Plan Task X.4 step 1 — the parity assertion fails the build if a column name is absent or differs (catches the stale `shows.snapshot_revision_id`/`shows.base_modified_time`/`last_attempted_at` class of names drifting between spec and plan). | Task X.6 | implemented | acceptance criterion row | Historical AC rows are represented in the matrix; X.6 parity assertions audit the cross-cutting AC body contracts. |
